title: Source & Evidence Review — tracking what supports each claim and what still needs review

fields.mechanism: Claims are inspected through a Foundational NSRP-style sourcing protocol, Claim Halo, North Doctrine, source and counter-source, warrant strength, West Dissent, judgment, South Revision, red-team pressure, certification, screen blueprint, data model, and live MuranoOS-oriented test scenarios rather than flattened into citations or comments.
fields.built_components: The full design-and-build package is complete: 17 documentation files, 6 SVG diagrams, 7 NSRP foundation work products, 30 files total at 213 KB compressed (~370 KB uncompressed). The Master Spec (27 sections), Screen Blueprint (8 screens, 12 artboards), Data Model (23 Postgres tables), GraphQL types, and Test Scenarios all clear the ?handoff complete? bar — a product team can derive screens and flows without guessing, a frontend team can identify modules and interactions, a platform team understands entities and persistence, and a governance owner sees gate logic and audit. The package is honest about what is not included: high-fidelity comps, live code, working graph database, production API integrations, real user research, a clickable prototype, and final institutional policy language. Those are downstream of the handoff, not part of it.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: The forward path moves the package from spec-level to live runtime. The page is designed to be implemented inside the MuranoOS desktop application; the next moves are the implementation, integration, and pilot scholarly use. Step 01 High-fidelity prototype Translate the screen blueprint and SVG diagrams into a working clickable prototype that exercises the Claim Halo interaction end-to-end. Step 02 Live MuranoOS integration Implement the 23-table schema in the MuranoOS persistence layer; wire the GraphQL types to the application layer; integrate the page into the MuranoOS desktop shell. Step 03 First scholarly use First real manuscript run through the page — every non-trivial claim graduated through the Claim Halo, every certification recorded against the role-permission matrix. Step 04 Tribunal cycle First full tribunal-layout cycle — committee or editorial-board review using the page as the working surface, with the full NSRP foundation operationalized in real review.
fields.status: Status: The full design-and-build package is complete: 17 documentation files, 6 SVG diagrams, 7 NSRP foundation work products, 30 files total at 213 KB compressed (~370 KB uncompressed). The Master Spec (27 sections), Screen Blueprint (8 screens, 12 artboards), Data Model (23 Postgres tables), GraphQL types, and Test Scenarios all clear the ?handoff complete? bar — a product team can derive screens and flows without guessing, a frontend team can identify modules and interactions, a platform team understands entities and.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: the forward path moves the package from spec-level to live runtime. The page is designed to be implemented inside the MuranoOS desktop application; the next moves are the implementation, integration, and pilot scholarly use.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A scholarly evidence-governance environment whose purpose is to preserve, display, and operationalize the full relation between text, claim, source, counter-source, judgment, repair, dissent, and certification — without flattening any of those layers into a bibliography, a comment thread, or a final score. The page is built for scholarship and strategic writing at a level where the user must be able to answer, for any non-trivial claim: what authorizes this statement, at what strength, with what limits, against what rivals, under whose judgment, and in which exact version of the text. Design target: elite doctoral, editorial-board, policy-board, or research-lab workflow. The page must feel consequential, ceremonial, and exact — inheriting nothing from generic SaaS dashboard conventions. Foundational NSRP (National-Security / Sourcing Protocol) is included losslessly so the page contains and operationalizes that prior protocol rather than replacing it.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The signature interaction is the Claim Halo. Selecting any claim opens a radial inspection interface organized around a five-zone warrant compass — each zone corresponds to a distinct epistemic task, and each is the kind of move a serious reader would otherwise have to assemble manually across separate surfaces.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: Claims are inspected through a Foundational NSRP-style sourcing protocol, Claim Halo, North Doctrine, source and counter-source, warrant strength, West Dissent, judgment, South Revision, red-team pressure, certification, screen blueprint, data model, and live MuranoOS-oriented test scenarios rather than flattened into citations or comments.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because expert writing needs to know what authorizes a claim, what contests it, what revision state carries it, what dissent has been faced, and what test would show the warrant is strong enough.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It matters because expert writing needs to know what authorizes a claim, what contests it, what revision state carries it, what dissent has been faced, and what test would show the warrant is strong enough.

instruments.input: draft claim, source, counter-source, intended audience
instruments.operation: opens the claim as a review object rather than treating it as finished prose; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
instruments.output: claim record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires draft claim, source, counter-source, intended audience
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds claim record as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: claim record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: The full design-and-build package is complete: 17 documentation files, 6 SVG diagrams, 7 NSRP foundation work products, 30 files total at 213 KB.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept draft claim, source, counter-source, intended audience and produce claim record as a reviewable artifact.
instruments.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (draft claim, source, counter-source, intended audience) produces output (claim record as a reviewable artifact) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.
